mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates an expanding economy, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market strength is a key coincident indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 50% of instances where the 3-month moving average of PAYEMS monthly change is positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate is above a restrictive level, borrowing costs for corporations and consumers are high, which slows economic activity and compresses equity valuation multiples, leading to negative returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed has held rates above 4.5% for over a year as of late 2007.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and a decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a sharp economic contraction where both labor and goods production are weakening. This joint signal of an impending recession causes negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Leading indicators for recessions often involve multiple series weakening together.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward Information Coefficient (IC) is not statistically different from zero over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle, high-interest-rate environment, a sharp year-over-year drop in housing starts (HOUST) is a leading indicator of a broader economic slowdown, as housing is a key interest-rate sensitive sector. This predicts negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The subprime mortgage crisis is evident in housing data throughout 2007.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances during a Fed tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A positive surprise in the monthly Industrial Production (INDPRO) release, relative to its 12-month trend, signals robust manufacturing and supply-side activity. This reflects strong corporate health and drives positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO monthly surprises and S&P 500 5-day forward returns is consistently non-positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, measured by the year-over-year change in CPI (CPIAUCSL), forces the central bank to maintain restrictive policy. This increases discount rates and uncertainty, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI has been volatile and above the Fed's informal target for parts of the last two years.
failure_condition: The signal has a positive hit rate (predicts negative returns correctly) of less than 55% during periods when YoY CPI is above 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates weakening consumer demand, which is a primary driver of corporate revenues. This negative signal for future earnings growth leads to negative stock market performance over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending constitutes the largest part of GDP.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the signal and 60-day forward returns is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high inflation (CPIAUCSL) and a restrictive Fed Funds rate (FEDFUNDS) creates a high probability of a policy-induced recession. This environment is highly negative for equities due to high discount rates and slowing growth expectations.
evidence_refs: doc:Analysis of the Volcker era recession.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances when the trigger condition is met.
